---
title: "Decision trees"
subtitle: "CART for regression and classification"
date: "Oct 30, 2026"
date-format: long
---

Recursive binary splitting; impurity measures (Gini, entropy); tree pruning via cost-complexity; trees as interpretable but high-variance learners.

📖 **Assigned reading:** ISLR §8.1

---

## 📊 Slides

- 🖥️ [Clean slides](slides_clean.pdf)
- ✏️ [Annotated slides](slides_annotated.pdf)  *(in-class version)*

## 📚 Readings

- 📄 [Breiman (2001)](readings/Breiman (2001).pdf)
- 📄 [Chen and Ishwaran (2012)](readings/Chen and Ishwaran (2012).pdf)
- 📄 [Watanabe et al. (2021)](readings/Watanabe et al. (2021).pdf)

## 🧪 Lab

In-class walkthrough — open the rendered lab in a new tab. The R Markdown source is provided so you can run the code yourself in RStudio.

- 🧪 [class10 lab1 (HTML)](code/class10_lab1.html){target="_blank"}
- 📝 [Lab source (class10_lab1.Rmd)](code/class10_lab1.Rmd)

## 💻 Code

**Figure scripts** — reproduce each figure from the slides:

- 📈 [fig1.R](code/fig1.R)
- 📈 [fig2.R](code/fig2.R)
- 📈 [fig3.R](code/fig3.R)
- 📈 [fig4.R](code/fig4.R)

**Interactive demos** — Shiny apps (run locally with `shiny::runApp()`):

- 🎛️ [app1.R](code/app1.R)
